data.title: Bitcoin Core Developers to Demonstrate 'Attack Blocks' on Signet Test Network
data.description: Bitcoin Core developers are set to showcase 'attack blocks' on Bitcoin's Signet test network at 10 AM EST on April 7. According to NS3.AI, these blocks are engineered to require significantly more time to verify compared to standard blocks. This demonstration aims to highlight a critical consensus vulnerability that the Great Consensus Cleanup seeks to resolve through BIP 54.

data.title: IMF Warns of Risks from Global Imbalances and Tariffs
data.description: IMF researchers have highlighted that tariffs rarely improve current accounts and are contributing to widening global imbalances, which pose risks to growth and financial stability. According to NS3.AI, the study indicates that permanent tariffs or retaliatory measures typically do not alter saving behaviors, leaving current account positions mostly unchanged. The IMF cautions that these growing imbalances have historically preceded financial crises or sudden reversals in capital flows.

data.title: The Iranian parliament's National Security Committee has begun deliberating on a plan to control the Strait of Hormuz.
data.description: Iranian Parliament's National Security Committee spokesman Ibrahim Rezaei said on the 6th that the committee has begun reviewing a proposal aimed at exercising Iranian sovereignty and establishing new arrangements and a legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz. The spokesman stated that a strategic action plan to ensure the security of the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf was on the agenda at this meeting, and some parts have already been reviewed and approved. After the National Security Committee completes its review, the proposal will be submitted to the plenary session of the Iranian Parliament for consideration. (CCTV)

data.title: Trump claims he destroyed Iranian bridge and power plant in 4 hours
data.description: On the afternoon of April 6 local time (early morning of April 7 Beijing time), US President Trump stated at a White House press conference that the US military "has actually developed a plan that, once activated, within four hours from 8 p.m. to midnight on April 7 Eastern Time, every bridge in Iran will be completely destroyed, every power plant in Iran will be completely paralyzed, engulfed in flames, exploded, and rendered unusable forever. Everything will be reduced to rubble, and the entire process will only take four hours." (CCTV International News)

data.title: Iran Reviews Plan for Legal Framework in Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Iran's Parliament National Security Committee has initiated the review of a proposal aimed at establishing sovereignty and a legal framework for the Strait of Hormuz. According to NS3.AI, spokesperson Ibrahim Rezaei stated that the plan encompasses strategic measures to ensure the security of both the Strait of Hormuz and the Persian Gulf. Upon completion of the review, the committee intends to forward the plan to the full parliament for further consideration.

data.title: BitMine Immersion Technologies Increases Ethereum Holdings with Significant Purchase
data.description: BitMine Immersion Technologies has acquired 71,252 Ethereum, valued at approximately $152 million, in the week ending April 5. According to NS3.AI, this acquisition has increased BitMine's total Ethereum holdings to 4.803 million, which is valued at around $10.3 billion and represents 3.98% of the circulating supply. The company has expressed its intention to accumulate 5% of the circulating supply. Following this purchase, shares of BMNR rose by about 6% on Monday.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Announces Potential Actions Against Iran
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has announced that all bridges in Iran are set to be destroyed by midnight on Tuesday. According to Odaily, he also stated that all power plants in the country will cease operations before midnight. If an agreement with Iran is not reached, these power plants will be dismantled. Trump emphasized that he hopes to avoid such actions.

data.title: Trump Criticizes NATO and Allies Over Iran Conflict Assistance
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump has expressed dissatisfaction with South Korea, Australia, Japan, and NATO regarding their lack of support in the ongoing conflict with Iran. According to NS3.AI, Trump labeled NATO as a "paper tiger," criticizing the alliance for its hesitance to provide assistance.

data.title: JPMorgan Maintains Sell Rating on Tesla with $145 Price Target
data.description: JPMorgan has reaffirmed its sell rating on Tesla, setting a price target of $145, which suggests a potential downside of approximately 60% from current levels. According to NS3.AI, Tesla's reported deliveries for the first quarter of 2026 amounted to 358,023 vehicles, falling short of Wall Street's expectations.

data.title: IMF Chief: War Impacts Economic Growth Projections
data.description: IMF Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva stated that without the ongoing war, the IMF had initially planned to slightly raise its 2026 economic growth forecast of 3.3%. According to Jin10, Georgieva emphasized that current circumstances indicate a trend towards higher prices and slower growth.

data.title: US President Trump: I would rather the US collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz than let Iran do it.
data.description: US President Trump: I'd rather the US collect tolls in the Strait of Hormuz than let Iran do it. (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. President Trump Prefers U.S. to Collect Fees in Hormuz Strait
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump expressed his preference for the United States to collect transit fees in the Strait of Hormuz rather than allowing Iran to do so. According to Odaily, Trump's statement highlights his stance on maintaining U.S. influence and control in the strategic waterway.

data.title: US President Trump: All power plants will be dismantled if no deal is reached with Iran.
data.description: US President Trump: All bridges in Iran will be destroyed by midnight Tuesday; all power plants will be shut down by midnight. If no agreement is reached with Iran, all power plants will be dismantled. We don't want that to happen. (Jinshi)

data.title: Polymarket Traders Face Losses as Study Reveals 84.1% Are Losing Money
data.description: Independent on-chain analyst Andrey Sergeenkov has conducted a study revealing that a significant portion of Polymarket traders are experiencing financial losses. According to NS3.AI, the analysis examined 2.5 million Polygon wallet addresses, uncovering that only 840 addresses have managed to earn $100,000 through trading on the platform. This finding highlights the challenges faced by traders in navigating the complexities of the market.

data.title: OpenServ Introduces SERV Nano with Promises of Enhanced Efficiency
data.description: OpenServ has unveiled its latest AI infrastructure platform, SERV Nano, highlighting its potential advantages over models associated with OpenAI. According to NS3.AI, the company claims that SERV Nano offers a 20-fold reduction in costs and a threefold increase in speed for certain tasks. The platform integrates Base and Solana rails to facilitate token launch and monetization. However, independent public verification of benchmark methods and specific deployments is currently limited.

data.title: Alphabet Stock Faces Potential Challenges Amid CPI and Oil Price Concerns
data.description: Alphabet's stock outlook this week is influenced by the upcoming U.S. Consumer Price Index (CPI) release on April 10 and Brent crude oil prices remaining above $109. According to NS3.AI, the stock's $290 support level may be tested if the CPI exceeds 2.4%. Additionally, oil prices above $110 could disrupt the recent stock rebound. Investors are also closely monitoring volume trends ahead of Alphabet's Q1 earnings call scheduled for April 23.

data.title: Senate Banking Committee to Address Digital Asset Market Structure Bill in April
data.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty anticipates that the Senate Banking Committee will consider a digital asset market structure bill during the upcoming work period, aiming to advance it out of committee by April. According to NS3.AI, the bill requires further action from both the banking and agriculture committees. Progress has been hindered by disagreements over tokenized equities, ethical concerns, and stablecoin yield.

data.title: Brazil Faces Tight Labor Market and Uncontrolled Inflation Expectations
data.description: Brazilian officials have expressed concerns over the country's current economic conditions, highlighting a very tight labor market. According to Jin10, inflation expectations remain in an uncontrolled state, posing significant challenges for economic stability. The situation underscores the need for strategic measures to address these pressing issues.

data.title: Home Loan Approval Criteria: Income and Debt Considerations
data.description: Securing a $1 million home loan typically requires an annual salary of approximately $175,000. According to NS3.AI, banks also assess credit scores and total debt levels during the approval process. If debt exceeds 36% of income, it may hinder loan approval.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Meta Platforms to Release Next-Generation AI Model
data.description: Meta Platforms is set to release the open-source version of its next-generation AI model. According to Jin10, this move is part of Meta's ongoing efforts to advance artificial intelligence technology and make it more accessible to developers and researchers. The release is expected to provide new opportunities for innovation and collaboration within the AI community.
